MeMurry, demurrage supervisor for the Frisco lines in North Texas, also lives in Fort In 1880 he was appointed captain of the ranger service and moved his head- quarters to Colorado City, Texas, where cattle rustling then was common and Worth.

headlines.title: CATTLE AND MEAT IMPORTS DOUBLED UNDER NEW TARIFF
headlines.fullText: By Associated Press. WASHINGTON, Jan, %-The free listing of beef and other food products under the new tariff law is causing enough increases in the importationa of foodstuffs Into the United States, Judging from figures Issued today by the department of commerce. Food importations in November Showed a marked increase over Im- ports for the same month a year and. paga conmastable cream five and two-thirds million pounds in na waz aires ma doubled in November, when 10.556,516 pounds of fresh beef were Importe More than one-half one half of the beef Imports came di- 000 pounds of pork and 32,000 pounds of matton Imported. Cattle importations in November numbered 123.118 head, valued at $3,- 206.723 against 42.768 head, valued at $829,358 in November, 1912. The total number of catue Importes in October and November under the new tariff law was 253,767. Importations of oats in November were 6.132.308 bushels. against 4.266,000 bushels in November, 1912. Importations of potatoes were 764,- :5 bushels, against 10,668 bushels in November, 1912.

headlines.title: CROSSING ORDINANCE LOSES AT WAXAHACHIE
headlines.subtitle: Suits Against Katy and T. & B. V. Won by Roads, To Be Appealed.
headlines.fullText: Ry Associated Press. WAXAHACHIE, Texas, Jan. 9-An ordinance passed by the city council a few years ago requiring railroads to today held in- valld by District Judge Hawkins. The decision was in suits by the city against the Katy and the Trinity & Brazos Valley to recover penalties from $13,600. It was alleged each road had been violating the or- dinance nearly two years. The case will be appealed.

headlines.title: Woman Justice of Peace Weds Chief of Police
headlines.fullText: LAPORTAandated Press. Jan. 3. Miss Clara Hess, Indiana's only woman Justice of early today to William Anstelas, who www . two days ago was appointed chief of pollce of Laporte. Mrs. Anstelss was a justice of the peace at Westyills. ind., where she has a country home. She is also prominent in lodge circles of the state.

headlines.title: Eugenic Law Is Causing Merchants to Lose Money
headlines.fullText: MILWAUKEE, Jan. 9 .- Merchants in this city who make a business of fur- nishing homes for newly-married cou- ples, today estimated that the dearth of marriages resulting from the new Wisconsin eugenics law. is costing them nearly $10,000 a day. Only five marriage licenses have been Issued in this city since the me Hme issued en that time the county clerk Issued on an average of twenty-five to thirty licenses a day.

Several witnesses are to be examined and the trial probably will inst throughout the day. Dr. W. C. headlines.title: CITY FINDS NEW WAY TO CUT BRIDGE COST
headlines.subtitle: Officials and County Commissioners Agree on Plan for "Nine-Mile" Structure.
headlines.fullText: At a conference between Mayor Mi- lam, City Commissioner Smith, Chair- man Trammell of the engineer board and the county commissioners, Friday. Nine-3tlle bridge above Lake Worth was agreed to by the county commis- The new plan, lesa costly than that Formerly proposed, is made by formerly programade te Wisconsin concern, and etty omnejals were in conference with a represent- ative of the company Friday, endeav- oring to reach a satisfactory agree- ment as to the price of the work. The plans proposed are said to be entirely satisfactory to the county officials,

headlines.title: TO HOLD PRIMARY
headlines.subtitle: Indiana Democrats to Follow New Law's Provisions.
headlines.fullText: INDIANAPOLIS, Jan. 2 .- Indiana Democrats announced last night that the party's state convention, March 12. would nominate a candidate for United constitutional amendment providing for the direct election of United States wnators Senator B. F. Shively is a candidate po for the nomination and bo opposition to him is expected, It was said.

headlines.title: MEXICAN SLAYER OF SIX SEEN IN LOS ANGELES
headlines.subtitle: Ralph Lopez. Reported in California by Fellow Countryman Who Once Worked With Him.
headlines.fullText: LOS ANGELES, Jan. 9. Ralph Lo- pez, the Mexican outlaw, who last No- vember killed six men and escaped from several sheriff# posses in the Utah-Apex mine at Bingham, Utah, was In Los Angeles yesterday, according to Information received at the sheriff's office. He Is said to have been recognized on the streets by a fellow countryman who once worked in a mine with the desperado.

headlines.title: $396,000 PAID
headlines.subtitle: Report Made on Compensation Law in Wisconsin.
headlines.fullText: MADISON, WiR. Jan. 9. In the Inst two years since the workingmen's com- pensation law went into effect, employ- ers of the state have paid an Indemnity to injured workmen and their depend- ents $196,754.83, according to an an- nouncement today by state Industrial commission, which has compiled the statistics. The amount does not include the sum pald under the mesterimmer provola equal 50 per cent of the amount paid as Indemnity.

headlines.title: SINGLE TAX ADVOCATE TO GIVE SPEECH HERE
headlines.subtitle: Joseph Fels of Philadelphia Will Visit Fort Worth in February, Says Notice to Mayor Milam Malam.
headlines.fullText: use of Pels- Nanthan and Humle tax advocate, will visit Fort Worth in February for the purpose of making an address on the single tax. according to notification received by Mayor Milam from J. J. Pastoriza, fi- nance commissioner of Houston. The single tax plan calls for taxation only on land and not upon the Improve- ments. Its advocates urge that the system prevents the holding of prop- erty for speculation and results in more rapid city building.
